family_name: Closed-neighborhood balanced colorings of quintic circulants
strongest_honest_claim: For the open n ≡ 0 (mod 4) quintic CNBC regime left outside the 2025 canonical paper, the strongest honest claim remains a theorem-slice candidate rather than a classification result: the family Lean backend now preserves the CNB-to-kernel translation for the six-term mask together with the Route I obstruction skeleton reducing nonexistence to the absence of a nonzero integer kernel vector, but the six-term distinctness, Fourier diagonalization, and nowhere-zero-symbol lemmas are not yet repo-locally checked in this worktree because Lake cannot bootstrap mathlib here.
paper_title_hint: A spectral obstruction for open quintic CNBC circulants with an antipodal generator
theorem_slice_target: Preserve and check the Route I family package in Lean: the CNB-to-kernel translation for the six-term quintic mask and the theorem-slice skeleton reducing nonexistence to the absence of a nonzero integer kernel vector; after repo-local Lake bootstrap, extend this to the six-term distinctness, Fourier diagonalization, and nowhere-zero-symbol lemmas and then repackage C_16(4,5,8) and C_20(2,3,10) as direct applications.
fallback_target: If Route I still cannot absorb C_20 cleanly, publish a narrower spectral-obstruction slice with C_16(4,5,8) as the clean direct application, keep the opposite-pair reduction as a reusable lemma for C_20(2,3,10), and treat C_24(4,5,12) only as a separate cube-root periodicity corollary/program rather than a co-headline theorem.
next_blocker: Repo-local Lake bootstrap is still blocking honest checking in this worktree: lean/.lake/packages is empty, and both `lake env lean` and `lake build` immediately try to clone mathlib4 and fail under network-disabled execution. After bootstrap, the next proof blockers are the six-term distinctness/cardinality lemma, the family Fourier diagonalization lemma, the nowhere-zero-symbol-to-trivial-kernel implication, and the clean Route I repackaging of C_20(2,3,10).
next_action: Materialize the repo-local Lake and mathlib dependencies in `lean/.lake/packages`, then run `cd lean && lake build AutoMath.Families.CNBCQuinticRouteI`; only after that honest check should the campaign close the six-term distinctness/cardinality lemma and the Fourier/nowhere-zero-symbol obstruction before spending the next feeder on c28-2-3-14-cnbc.
